name: Fluffy Pancakes
description: Golden, tender pancakes with a crispy exterior. Perfect weekend breakfast in 20 minutes.
steps: Whisk together flour, sugar, baking powder, and salt in a large bowl.
In another bowl, beat eggs and whisk in milk, melted butter, and vanilla.
Pour wet ingredients into dry and stir until just combined. Don't overmix; lumps are fine.
Heat a buttered skillet or griddle over medium heat until a drop of water dances on the surface.
Pour 1/4 cup batter per pancake and cook until edges look set and bubbles form on top, about 2–3 minutes.
Flip and cook the other side until golden brown, about 1–2 minutes.

name: Avocado Toast
description: Creamy, rich, and ready in minutes. A weekday breakfast that feels intentional.
steps: Toast bread to your liking—golden and crisp but not burnt.
While bread toasts, cut avocado in half, remove the pit, and scoop into a small bowl.
Mash avocado lightly with a fork. Squeeze in lemon juice and season with salt and pepper.
Spread avocado mixture generously on toast.
Drizzle with olive oil, add a pinch of red pepper flakes if you like heat, and serve immediately.

name: Shakshuka
description: Eggs poached in a spiced tomato sauce with Middle Eastern warmth. Scoopable, satisfying, and deeply savory.
steps: Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add diced onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add minced garlic and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Pour in crushed tomatoes, cumin, and paprika. Stir well and simmer for 8–10 minutes, breaking up tomatoes. Season with salt and pepper.
Make 4 small wells in the sauce with the back of a spoon.
Crack an egg into each well. Reduce heat to low, cover, and cook for 6–8 minutes until eggs are set to your liking.
Garnish with fresh herbs and serve hot with crusty bread for scooping.

name: Grilled Salmon
description: Flaky, buttery salmon with a crispy skin and subtle char. Simple, elegant, and ready in 20 minutes.
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
2 teaspoons fresh dill, or 1 teaspoon dried
steps: Preheat your grill to medium-high heat and oil the grates to prevent sticking.
Pat salmon dry and brush lightly with olive oil. Season generously with salt and pepper.
Place salmon skin-side down on the grill. Cook for 5–6 minutes without moving.
Flip carefully and cook for another 3–4 minutes until the flesh is opaque and flakes easily.
Transfer to a plate, squeeze fresh lemon over top, and sprinkle with dill.

name: Pasta Carbonara
description: Creamy, peppery, pure Italian simplicity. Just eggs, cheese, and pasta—done right, it's magic.
ingredients: 4 large eggs, separated yolks from whites
steps: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil and cook pasta until just al dente.
While pasta cooks, render pancetta in a large skillet over medium heat until crispy. Remove from heat.
Whisk egg yolks with grated cheese and a generous amount of black pepper in a bowl.
Reserve 1 cup pasta water, then drain pasta.
Add hot pasta to the skillet with pancetta. Toss off heat for 30 seconds.
Pour egg-cheese mixture over pasta and toss constantly, adding pasta water a splash at a time until creamy and silky. Serve immediately.

name: Chicken Stir-Fry
description: Tender chicken with crisp vegetables in a savory sauce. Ready in 30 minutes and tastes way better than takeout.
ingredients: 1.5 pounds boneless, skinless chicken breast, sliced into strips
2 tablespoons rice wine or rice vinegar
steps: Mix together soy sauce, rice wine, sesame oil, and cornstarch in a small bowl. Set aside.
Heat 1 tablespoon oil in a large skillet or wok over high heat. Working in batches, cook chicken until golden and cooked through, about 3–4 minutes per batch. Remove to a plate.
Add remaining 2 tablespoons oil to the pan. Add bell pepper, snap peas, and carrots. Stir-fry for 4–5 minutes until crisp-tender.
Add garlic and ginger, cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Return chicken to the pan. Pour in sauce and toss constantly for 1–2 minutes until everything is coated and glossy.
Serve over rice.

name: Chocolate Lava Cake
description: Warm, molten chocolate center with a tender cake exterior. Fancy enough for dinner guests, easy enough for a weeknight.
ingredients: Butter and cocoa powder for ramekins
steps: Preheat your oven to 425°F. Butter two 6-ounce ramekins and dust lightly with cocoa powder.
Melt chocolate and butter together in a heat-proof bowl over simmering water, or in 30-second microwave bursts, stirring between each.
In another bowl, whisk eggs, sugar, salt, and vanilla until thick and pale, about 2 minutes.
Fold the warm chocolate mixture into the eggs gently, then fold in flour until just combined.
Divide batter between ramekins and place on a baking sheet.
Bake for 10–12 minutes until the edges are firm but the center jiggles slightly when you gently shake the ramekin.
Run a knife around the edges and invert onto dessert plates. Serve immediately, optionally with vanilla ice cream.

name: Lemon Bars
description: Buttery shortbread base topped with bright, tart-sweet lemon custard. One of the few desserts that tastes like sunshine.
steps: Preheat oven to 350°F. Line a 9x13-inch baking pan with parchment paper.
Mix softened butter, 2 cups flour, and 1/2 cup powdered sugar. Press firmly into the prepared pan.
Bake the base for 12–15 minutes until golden and set.
While base bakes, whisk together eggs, granulated sugar, 1/4 cup flour, baking powder, and salt.
Stir in lemon juice and zest until combined.
Pour lemon filling over the hot base and bake for 20–25 minutes until the top is set but still slightly jiggly in the very center.
Cool completely, then dust generously with powdered sugar. Cut into squares and serve.
